primary_complaint: Right shoulder pain and weakness following a work-related lifting injury
functional_limitations: Unable to lift objects above shoulder height
Cannot perform overhead reaching for daily tasks
Disturbed sleep due to shoulder pain
Unable to return to work duties requiring arm elevation
objective_measurements: Active forward flexion: 95 degrees (limited by pain)
Strength testing: 3/5 supraspinatus on empty can test

conservative_treatments_attempted.outcome: minimal improvement, pain persisted with overhead activity
2 weeks of partial relief, then return to baseline symptoms
inadequate pain control, GI intolerance developed

imaging_findings.key_findings: X-ray (01/22/2025): AC joint arthrosis Grade II. No calcific deposits. No significant glenohumeral arthrosis.; MRI (01/22/2025): Full-thickness supraspinatus tear measuring 2.1 cm with retraction to the musculotendinous junction. Moderate subacromial bursitis. Intact subscapularis.

requested_procedure: Arthroscopic rotator cuff repair, right shoulder - CPT 29827
surgical_approach_if_mentioned: Arthroscopic with mini-open rotator cuff repair

denial_risk_flags.label: Physical Therapy Duration Below Typical Threshold
denial_risk_flags.explanation: Physical therapy was documented for only 8 weeks. Many BCBS policies for CPT 29827 require a minimum of 6–12 weeks of formal PT, but reviewers may scrutinize whether 8 weeks is sufficient given the severity of the tear and the short duration relative to some payer thresholds.
denial_risk_flags.recommendation: Add documentation clarifying the number of PT sessions attended, the specific exercises performed, and objective measurements at start and end of PT to demonstrate a structured, supervised program was completed and failed.

denial_risk_flags.label: Corticosteroid Injection Duration Not Documented
denial_risk_flags.explanation: The corticosteroid injection entry does not include a duration of treatment or a follow-up interval, only a single administration date placeholder (11/05/2024). Payers may flag incomplete conservative care timelines as insufficient to establish failure.
denial_risk_flags.recommendation: Add a follow-up note dated after 11/05/2024 documenting the patient's return to baseline symptoms and confirming the injection provided only 2 weeks of partial relief before surgical planning was initiated.

denial_risk_flags.label: Work Injury Origin May Require Workers' Comp Coordination
denial_risk_flags.explanation: The chart documents the injury as work-related, which may trigger a workers' compensation coordination-of-benefits review by BCBS before authorizing the procedure under the health plan.
denial_risk_flags.recommendation: Confirm whether a workers' compensation claim has been filed or denied. If denied or not applicable, include documentation of that determination in the prior authorization submission to prevent a COB-related denial.

denial_risk_flags.label: Attending Provider Name Is a Placeholder
denial_risk_flags.explanation: The attending surgeon is listed as Dr. Michael Torres in the chart, which is a placeholder token rather than a verified provider name. Payers require a credentialed, identified requesting provider for prior authorization approval.
denial_risk_flags.recommendation: Ensure the attending surgeon's full legal name, NPI, and BCBS credentialing information are populated before submission. Confirm Dr. Michael Torres resolves to the correct provider on file.

diagnosis_codes.note: M75.121 (full-thickness rotator cuff tear, right shoulder) is a clinically appropriate and direct medical necessity indication for CPT 29827 arthroscopic rotator cuff repair.

conservative_treatment_duration.note: 2 of 2 duration-eligible treatments have an explicit numeric duration (100%). Meets the 50% threshold.

surgical_approach.note: The surgical approach is explicitly documented as 'Arthroscopic with mini-open rotator cuff repair,' which is a specific, procedure-appropriate technique for CPT 29827.

cpt_code_valid.note: CPT 29827 is a recognized orthopedic surgical code.
